name: Tour of the Villages of Segrià
name_original: Vuelta Circular por la llanura de Alguaire
description: Enjoy an easy 18.2-mile road cycling tour through the Alguaire Plain, featuring agricultural landscapes and charming villages.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alpicat traverse a diverse landscape characterized by agricultural heartlands, vineyards, and open plains. The terrain offers a mix of flat sections and rolling hills, providing varied experiences for cyclists. The region's proximity to the Segre River also contributes to scenic routes, often featuring quiet, paved roads suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Alpicat The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the Villages of Segrià, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This easy route features gentle elevation through the agricultural landscapes of Segrià. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Path – Raimat vineyards loop from Alpicat, a moderate 33.0 miles (53.1 km) path. This route winds through the notable Raimat vineyards and surrounding agricultural areas. Local road cyclists also love the View of Lleida – Airport Route loop from Rosselló, a 30.5 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through open plains with views towards Lleida, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Alpicat is defined by agricultural heartlands, vineyards, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Alpicat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 34 reviews. More than 5,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alpicat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alpicat?
A: Alpicat and its surrounding areas offer a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 240 options available. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Alpicat?
A: The terrain around Alpicat is diverse, characterized by rich agricultural heartlands, vineyards, and open plains. You'll find a mix of flat sections and rolling hills, with mostly paved and generally quiet roads. For more experienced riders, the region also offers access to higher-altitude climbs towards the Pyrenees.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Alpicat?
A: The road cycling routes in Alpicat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 34 reviews. More than 5,800 road cyclists have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain, quiet roads, and scenic agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Alpicat?
A: Yes, Alpicat offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944692018/tour-of-the-villages-of-segria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Villages of Segrià&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.2-mile (29.3 km) trail featuring gentle elevation through agricultural landscapes. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noguera Ribagorçana – Parque de la Mitjana loop from Rosselló&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: While Alpicat itself features more moderate terrain, the broader Lleida region provides access to more challenging rides. The area's proximity to the Pyrenees allows for impressive high-altitude road cycling with climbs that can rival those in the Alps, often with fewer crowds. Routes like the 'Fucked Dutch - Route 1' (103 km with 1,024m elevation gain) offer more extensive rides.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Alpicat?
A: Road cycling routes in Alpicat often pass through scenic agricultural lands, including vineyards and almond groves. You might also encounter natural attractions related to the Segre River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge&lt;/a&gt; or the green spaces of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5581211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de la Mitjana&lt;/a&gt;, which is also great for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, many routes wind through historical towns and villages, allowing you to experience local culture. Notable landmarks in the vicinity include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4606595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Paeria Palace&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4972766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu Vella de Lleida&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are accessible from various cycling paths.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Alpicat?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature in the Alpicat area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20178032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path – Raimat vineyards loop from Alpicat&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 33-mile (53.1 km) ride through vineyards and agricultural areas. The wider Lleida plain also features scenic circular routes like the 'Terres de Lleida circular loop'.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alpicat?
A: The region generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild, allowing for year-round cycling with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there options for parking when road cycling in Alpicat?
A: Yes, Alpicat and the surrounding towns offer various parking options. Many routes start from easily accessible points in or near villages, where you can typically find public parking. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Can I reach Alpicat and its cycling routes using public transport with my bicycle?
A: Public transport options, such as buses from Lleida, may allow bicycles, though policies can vary. It's recommended to check with the specific bus operator (e.g., Lleida to Alpicat bus services) regarding their bicycle transport regulations and any potential restrictions or additional fees before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any bike shops in Alpicat or nearby for repairs or gear?
A: While Alpicat is a smaller town, the nearby city of Lleida offers a wider range of services, including bike shops where you can find repairs, spare parts, and cycling gear. It's always a good idea to ensure your bike is in good condition before heading out on a ride.
tips: Alguaire is a town and municipality in the Segrià region. It is located at the foot of a wall on which the Castle stood and is surrounded by agricultural fields and an airport, that of the capital, Lleida.
Benavent de Segrià is a municipality in the Segrià region, located in the Segrian plain in the north of Lleida and in the sector next to the right of the Noguera Ribagorçana and Segre rivers.
La Grallera is a town that has now disappeared where you can currently see some vestiges of its passage through La Grallera.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Alguaire, you can typically find parking within the town of Alguaire itself. Look for public parking areas or street parking that is permitted.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain is rated as 'easy'. The terrain across the Lleida Plain is generally flat to gently rolling, making it ideal for road cyclists of various fitness levels, including beginners looking for a leisurely ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat to gently rolling, characteristic of the broader Lleida Plain. You'll be cycling on quiet, well-surfaced roads that pass through expansive agricultural landscapes, including fruit orchards and cereal fields.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views of vast agricultural landscapes, charming villages, and historical elements. You'll pass through Alguaire, and notable sights include the Church of Benavent de Segrià and the historical site of La Grallera.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain is approximately 29.3 km (18.2 miles) long. On average, it takes about 1 hour and 28 minutes to complete, depending on your cycling pace.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Alguaire Plain?
faqs.answer: The Lleida Plain, where Alguaire is located, is excellent for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. These seasons also offer beautiful views of the agricultural landscapes, with orchards in bloom or harvest.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, sections of it overlap with local paths. You'll briefly encounter parts of the Pla de Ponent, Camí de les Fonecades, and Passeig de Pau Casals.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alguaire, where you can find cafes and restaurants. As you cycle through the agricultural plain, options may be limited, so it's advisable to carry water and snacks.

faqs.question: Is the Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally welcome in public spaces, it's not ideal for dogs to run alongside a road bike. If you wish to bring a dog, a bicycle trailer would be the most suitable option.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Circular Tour of the Alguaire Plain. It's an open route accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What makes this area a good choice for road cycling?
faqs.answer: The Lleida Plain stands out for its quiet, well-surfaced roads and diverse agricultural landscapes. It offers a less crowded alternative to more famous cycling destinations, providing a peaceful experience suitable for all skill levels, combined with cultural heritage.
